_description: Default structure selection behaviour. Overridable via user profile or session.

vanilla_call.optimised_for: Directional upside with full participation; no cap on gains

vanilla_put.optimised_for: Directional downside with full participation; no cap on gains

call_spread.optimised_for: Capped directional upside at reduced premium; ideal when target level is known

put_spread.optimised_for: Capped directional downside at reduced premium; ideal when target level is known

risk_reversal.optimised_for: Zero or near-zero premium directional expression; sells vol in opposite direction to fund the bought leg
risk_reversal.caution: Unlimited loss on sold option leg; requires margin; skew makes one direction expensive

rko_call.optimised_for: Cheaper directional expression when PM believes spot will not trade through a specific topside level before reaching target
rko_call.caution: Option ceases to exist if spot touches the knock-out level; path matters, not just expiry

european_digital.optimised_for: Fixed binary payout if spot is through strike at expiry; high leverage on small moves near strike at expiry
european_digital.caution: Binary gamma near expiry is difficult to manage; payout is all-or-nothing

european_digital_rko.optimised_for: Further-reduced premium digital; contingent on spot not touching a barrier during the life
european_digital_rko.caution: Combines binary expiry risk with knock-out path risk; complex payout profile

decision_rules.description: High direction conviction, high or medium timing conviction — full directional expression
decision_rules.rationale: High conviction warrants full delta participation. Pay the premium for unrestricted upside.

decision_rules.description: Budget-constrained with known target level — spread structures
decision_rules.rationale: If PM has a specific target, capping the payout at that level is efficient. Spread reduces premium by selling the strike beyond target.

decision_rules.description: Buying into skew — buying topside in a topside-skewed currency
decision_rules.rationale: When the view aligns with the expensive side of skew, vanilla premium is elevated. A spread caps the cost. A risk reversal sells skew on the other side — only appropriate if PM is comfortable with the sold leg exposure.
decision_rules.skew_note: Risk reversal is second on shortlist, not first, when skew is high — sold leg exposure requires explicit PM acceptance.

decision_rules.description: Low timing conviction — simple structure with tranche entry
decision_rules.rationale: Low timing conviction is handled through the sizing discipline (tranche entry), not structure complexity. A simple vanilla allows adding to the position naturally.

decision_rules.description: Low direction conviction — no options, only spot or forward
decision_rules.rationale: Low direction conviction does not support paying options premium. Spot or forward gives exposure without the vol premium cost — stop discipline does the risk management work.
